input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: UQ marginally calibrated but conditional coverage at the COU subgroup fails: average coverage hides a subgroup the decision depends on.

case_context.cou: the intervals gate a fatigue decision driven by high-stress elements

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Marginal coverage averages over subgroups and reads nominal, but conditional coverage on the high-stress subgroup the COU depends on is far below nominal, so the UQ is not validated for the decision-relevant regime.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.scope: conditional coverage on the high-stress subgroup

forbidden_claims: asserts the intervals are wrong everywhere (a verdict beyond the subgroup gap)
treats marginal coverage as conditional coverage for the COU subgroup

hardness.obvious_signal: marginal (overall) interval coverage is 0.90 vs nominal 0.90 — calibration looks correct
hardness.conflicting_signal: conditional coverage on the high-stress subgroup the COU's fatigue decision depends on is only 0.62; marginal coverage averages over subgroups and hides the under-coverage where it matters

label_provenance.external_grounding: Conformal/UQ practice + ASME V&V 40: marginal coverage does not imply conditional coverage; the decision-relevant subgroup must be validated.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
